bead.title: readiness: align intake prompt with bead-lifecycle schema
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim readiness/intake hook is producing malformed `readiness_checks` shapes during live `ddx work` runs, including object-shaped and string-shaped values. Decoder hardening is tracked separately, but there is also a prompt/skill contract mismatch that encourages model drift: the bead-lifecycle skill documents a readiness schema with `readiness_checks` as an array, while the runtime prompt asks for a partially different field set.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31` say `MODE: intake` is compatibility readiness mode unless the caller gives a narrower schema.
- The same skill copies at lines 71-125 document the readiness JSON schema with `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, `readiness_checks` as an array of objects, `suggested_fixes`, `rewrite`, `suggested_child_beads`, and `waivers_applied`.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:242-248` builds the live pre-claim prompt with `MODE: intake` but asks for `classification, confidence, reasoning, readiness_checks, suggested_fixes, suggested_child_beads, and waivers_applied`; it omits `tractability`, `score`, and `rationale`, uses legacy `confidence/reasoning`, and also says not to include rewrite fields.
- This split contract leaves the model deciding which schema to follow, so malformed `readiness_checks` values can appear even when the skill itself shows an array example.

PROPOSED FIX
Align the live pre-claim prompt and all shipped bead-lifecycle skill copies around one explicit intake/readiness contract. Either make the runtime prompt quote the exact readiness-mode JSON shape DDx decodes, or make the skill define a deliberately narrower intake schema that exactly matches the runtime prompt. In either case, explicitly state that `readiness_checks` MUST be a JSON array, may be empty, and every entry MUST be an object with `reason`, `verdict`, `evidence`, and `checkable_before_attempt`. Keep the operator-required threshold strict: prompt-quality nits go in `suggested_fixes`; only true blockers become `operator_required`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not replace decoder hardening in ddx-c81c3cad; DDx must still tolerate malformed model output. Do not change readiness classifications or queue state-machine semantics. Do not make readiness failures block work by default.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema` or equivalent asserts the generated prompt contains the same field names and shape as the bead-lifecycle readiness schema, including `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, and `readiness_checks` as an array.
2. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_ForbidsScalarReadinessChecks` or equivalent asserts the generated prompt explicitly says `readiness_checks` MUST be a JSON array and must not be an object/string.
3. `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` remain synchronized for the readiness/intake JSON contract.
4. `ddx skills check .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle` passes, or the repo-equivalent skill validation command passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_.*|TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestReadiness.*" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

{"rationale":"Well-formed bug bead with clear problem, file:line root cause, numbered verification, and usable prompt context, but it is missing an explicit parent/deps declaration and its labels do not include an explicit cross-reference facet such as the governing spec or ADR.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Add an explicit parent field, or if this is intended to be a root bead, state `No deps.` explicitly in the metadata.","Add a cross-reference label such as `spec:FEAT-010` or `adr:023` so the label set satisfies the rubric's cross-ref requirement."],"waivers_applied":[]}
